South Africa • Southern Africa

Kruger National Park

A major Big Five safari zone with wide lodge choice and strong self-drive or guided options.

★ 4.8 913 reviews
Image slot Attraction Kruger National Park
Image slot Country South Africa
Image slot Region Southern Africa

About this attraction

Kruger is one of Africa’s most flexible safari products because travelers can choose public park camps, private reserves, or high-end concessions.

How to get there

Most routes begin via Johannesburg and continue by road or regional flight to Skukuza, Hoedspruit, or Nelspruit.

Best time to visit

May to September is the classic dry-season safari window.

Highlights

  • Independent landing page with a clear planning summary
  • Nearby accommodation and dining recommendations
  • External booking CTA connected to a partner page
  • Practical access and timing guidance before booking

Practical information

Use this page to understand arrival logistics, time commitment, where to stay nearby, and which local tour operators are best placed to support the experience.

Full description

Kruger is one of Africa’s most flexible safari products because travelers can choose public park camps, private reserves, or high-end concessions. Trek Africa Guide positions this as a practical planning page, so travelers can compare route logic, nearby stays, dining, and partner booking options before leaving the site.

Nearby stays

Accommodations near Kruger National Park

Image slot Stay Kruger Shalati

Luxury safari hotel

Kruger Shalati - The Train on the Bridge

A distinctive Kruger stay built around restored train carriages on the Selati Bridge over the Sabie River.

★ 4.7 (519) From $800 / night
Skukuza, Kruger National Park
View Details

Nearby dining

Restaurants near Kruger National Park

Image slot Restaurant Kruger Shalati Dining

Safari hotel dining

Kruger Shalati Dining

Dining at Kruger Shalati is part of the stay, with meals shaped around game drives and the Sabie River setting.

★ 4.5 (188) $$$$
Bridge hotel meals, bush breakfasts, and South African lodge dinners
